meta.description: A mobile app for finding natural springs and sharing their current water flow.

nav.menuDescription: Landing page navigation and links to download the app.

hero.eyebrow: A mobile app for trips and caring for the countryside
hero.title: Find the springs that are flowing right now
hero.description: Studánky shows springs near you, up-to-date water reports, and a simple way to add your own observations from the field.
hero.domainNote: Domain: {domain}. Store links are still placeholders for now.

items.label: ready for use in the field

problem.title: A spring is more than a pin on the map
problem.description: With water out in the countryside, what matters is the current state, trust, and an easy way to share what you saw on your visit.

points.title: Maps show the place, but not its current state
points.description: A spring can be beautifully marked and still not be flowing. The landing page has to quickly explain why an up-to-date report matters.

points.title: Information from the field gets lost
points.description: Visitors often know what's happening at a spring, but have no easy place to add that knowledge safely.

points.title: Caretakers and hikers need the same overview
points.description: One clear app can connect day-trippers, volunteers, and the people who look after a specific place.

features.title: Everything that matters before you stop at a spring
features.description: For now this section works as a skeleton for the final content, screenshots, and a connection to real data.
features.cardNote: Ready to be extended with a specific UI state of the app.

items.title: Spring map
items.description: Quickly find springs nearby and see whether they're worth a stop along your route.

items.title: Current water state
items.description: Reports from the field help you tell whether a spring is flowing and when someone last checked it.

items.title: Filters for your trip
items.description: Pick a place by accessibility, surroundings, and the details that matter for your journey.

items.title: Share observations
items.description: Easily add a new report and help other people and spring caretakers.

howItWorks.title: Three steps from the map to a useful report
howItWorks.description: This skeleton holds the workflow that will need to be shown alongside screenshots and microcopy.

steps.title: Open the map
steps.description: Find springs near you or at the spot of a planned trip.

steps.title: Check the state
steps.description: Look at the latest report and information about the water flow.

steps.title: Add a report
steps.description: After your visit, note whether the water is flowing and help the next visitors.

screenshots.title: Space for screenshots and a future animated mockup
screenshots.description: The carousel already uses Embla and supports swiping. At this stage it holds SVG placeholders that will later be replaced by real app screenshots.

items.title: Map of nearby springs
items.description: A clear way to search for places near you.
items.imageAlt: Spring map in the Studánky mobile app

items.title: Spring detail
items.description: Water state, location, description, and the latest reports in one place.
items.imageAlt: Spring detail in the Studánky mobile app

items.title: Adding a report
items.description: Quickly share information from the field.
items.imageAlt: Report form in the Studánky app

testimonials.title: A placeholder for credibility before launch
testimonials.description: Before launch, this is where specific quotes from testers, spring caretakers, or project partners belong.

items.quote: On trips I often look for water off the main route. Up-to-date reports save me pointless detours.
items.author: Hiker
items.context: hiking and trail running in nature

items.quote: With springs it's important to know what's happening right now. Shared reports give the map practical meaning.
items.author: Volunteer
items.context: caring for springs and their surroundings

faq.title: Questions for the first version of the landing page
faq.description: The FAQ is built as a shadcn Accordion on top of Base UI, i.e. with an array of values in defaultValue.

items.question: Is the app free?
items.answer: Basic use of both the landing page and the planned app assumes free access. The exact model will be specified before release.

items.question: Can I add my own water-state report?
items.answer: Yes. The architecture assumes reports from the field that help others learn a spring's current state.

items.question: Will Studánky work without a signal?
items.answer: For now the landing page only introduces the app. The app itself is meant to be designed with field use and poor connectivity in mind.

items.question: Where does the spring data come from?
items.answer: The project assumes a combination of existing data, our own backend, and user reports. Detailed sources will be described before the public launch.

finalCta.title: Download Studánky to your phone
finalCta.description: The final version will add live App Store and Google Play links, a QR code, and click tracking.
finalCta.qrNote: QR placeholder for quickly opening the link on mobile.
finalCta.qrAlt: QR code to download the Studánky app
